data.description:
## Why

When Story 1 detects code drift, the default remedy is a FULL re-review (~3-5 min, billable, ~12
LLM criteria + a broad open-ended pass). That is wasteful: a CODE change can only affect the review
elements that actually reason about code. The plan-text criteria (e.g. "is each AC measurable"),
the broad unknown-unknowns pass, and the child-coverage criteria are **code-blind by construction**
— rebar already ENFORCES this (`registry.py`: `CODEBASE_GROUNDED = frozenset({"E4","G1G2","A1","G6"})`,
"only these AGENT-tier criteria grep/read the live codebase — the orchestrator enforces it; no other
criterion greps"). So on pure code drift their cached findings are provably unaffected and can be
reused. As Joe put it: a change to the ticket's file is 100% impacting for code-grounded elements,
but may impact zero non-code-related review elements.

This is incremental re-verification (proof-caching dependency checksums; Bazel partial rebuild) done
the SOUND way — narrowing along a static, harness-enforced partition, NOT along fragile per-commit
attribution or "what the review happened to cite."

## What

- **Narrowed re-review on pure code drift (ticket material UNCHANGED):** re-run only `{P2, P3}` (the
  cheap deterministic symbol/file resolvers) ∪ the applicable members of `CODEBASE_GROUNDED`
  (`{E4, G1G2, A1, G6}` filtered by `applies_at`). Reuse all other cached findings — they cannot have
  read code (orchestrator-enforced), so code drift cannot have staled them (this includes the
  expensive `BROAD` unknown-unknowns pass and `G3`/`G4` child-coverage, which read the ticket GRAPH,
  not code).
- **Re-aggregate Pass-3** over the FULL merged finding set. A new BLOCK from a re-run criterion flips
  the verdict (fail-closed); never short-circuit "it was passing before."
- **Chained attestation:** emit a new attestation whose SIGNED payload records each finding's
  code-hash vintage + the reuse map, so a single signature never launders a verdict partly derived
  from a prior code state. Remains externally CI-verifiable.
- **Trust the enforced partition:** treat `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` as authoritative; do not re-derive a
  separate "provably code-blind" set.
- **Fail-safe to FULL re-review on registry / criteria-contract version skew** between sign time and
  re-review time (the partition assumption may have changed). (Ticket-material change is already
  handled by Story 1 / the existing material-fingerprint full-invalidation path.)

### Explicitly descoped (decided in brainstorm)

- **Observed read-set capture** — rejected: a reviewer reading a file does NOT imply the file is
  load-bearing for the ticket; capturing reads casts too wide a net and would trigger erroneous
  re-reviews. The dependency basis stays Story 1's proxy set (`file_impact` ∪ citations).
- **`>N files drifted` → full re-review** — rejected as arbitrary: impact is about which review
  elements a change touches, not the file count.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py` — partial-run path (re-run only `{P2,P3}` ∪ applicable
  `CODEBASE_GROUNDED`) + Pass-3 partial re-aggregation over the merged set.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` — chained attestation (per-finding code-hash vintage +
  reuse map in the signed manifest) + version-skew detection.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py` — `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` as the partition authority + a
  registry/contract version stamp for skew detection.
- Tests under the plan-review test suite.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] On code drift with ticket material unchanged, ONLY `{P2,P3}` ∪ applicable
      `{E4,G1G2,A1,G6}` re-run; all other findings are reused from the prior attestation.
- [ ] Pass-3 re-aggregates over the merged finding set; a new BLOCK from a re-run criterion flips the
      verdict (fail-closed).
- [ ] The chained attestation records per-finding code-hash vintage + the reuse map in the SIGNED
      payload and is externally CI-verifiable.
- [ ] Registry / criteria-contract version skew between sign and re-review forces a FULL re-review.
- [ ] Descope is documented in code/docs: no observed-read-set capture; no `>N files` fallback.
- [ ] Measurement: report the token/latency saving vs a full re-review on a representative ticket
      before enabling narrowed re-review by default (the 4 expensive code agents still re-run; the
      saving is the cheap text criteria + non-code agent-tier + `BROAD`).
data.title: Narrowed delta-re-review on code drift: re-run only the enforced code-grounded criteria with a chained attestation
